skill.name: react-doctor
skill.description: Runs React Doctor after React code changes to catch security, performance, correctness, and architecture issues early.
skill.summary: Run a React project health scan after code changes and review prioritized diagnostics.
skill.author: millionco

security_audit.summary: The external command finding is a true positive because the skill instructs users to run an npm package through npx with the latest tag. The weak cryptography finding is a false positive because SKILL.md line 3 is description text and contains no cryptographic code. No prompt injection or data exfiltration instructions were found.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned npm Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to run npx -y react-doctor@latest against the current project. This is legitimate for a React diagnostic tool, but it downloads and executes the latest npm package version and grants it project filesystem access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution is explicit and directly supported by the skill instructions. The risk is moderate because the intent is legitimate, but the package version is unpinned and runs over the project directory.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 3, but that line is only frontmatter description text. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or encryption code appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 was manually reviewed and contains only a natural-language description. There is no semantic evidence of cryptographic behavior in the skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Package Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The usage instructions execute react-doctor through npx with the latest tag. Users should prefer a reviewed or pinned version when running this in sensitive repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx invocation is plainly present and matches the external command risk factor. The command appears intended for legitimate diagnostics, so the concern is supply-chain exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

content.user_title: Run React Health Checks After Code Changes
content.value_statement: React changes can introduce hidden security, performance, and correctness issues. React Doctor helps run a focused project scan and returns prioritized diagnostics.
actual_capabilities: Runs React Doctor against the current React project.
Checks for security, performance, correctness, and architecture issues.
Produces a 0-100 project score for quick triage.
Returns actionable diagnostics for follow-up fixes.
Supports post-change review and verification workflows.
limitations: Requires Node.js and npm tooling to run the npx command.
May download the latest npm package version unless users pin a version.
Does not replace unit tests, integration tests, or manual review.
Results depend on the behavior of the react-doctor npm package.

use_cases.title: Review a Feature Branch
use_cases.description: Run a React Doctor scan after feature work and prioritize the most important diagnostics before review.

use_cases.title: Check a Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Verify that a React bug fix did not add new correctness, security, or performance issues.

use_cases.title: Prepare Pull Request Feedback
use_cases.description: Use the scan results to guide focused code review comments and follow-up tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Scan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check after a small React change
prompt_templates.prompt: Run React Doctor for this project and summarize the main diagnostics in priority order.

prompt_templates.title: Review Recent Changes
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of a feature branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Run React Doctor with diff-focused output, then explain which findings affect the current change set.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Fix Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Run React Doctor and create a concise fix plan that separates errors, warnings, and follow-up improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Improvements
prompt_templates.prompt: Run React Doctor, apply the necessary fixes, rerun the scan, and compare the before and after results.

output_examples.input: Run a React Doctor scan after updating a component.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review summary with the project score, key issues, affected areas, and recommended next fixes.

output_examples.input: Check whether my recent React bug fix introduced regressions.
output_examples.output: A focused diagnostic report that separates blocking problems from lower-priority performance or architecture concerns.

output_examples.input: Help me prepare pull request feedback from React Doctor results.
output_examples.output: A concise review note with actionable items, suggested owners, and verification steps for the next scan.

best_practices: Run the scan after meaningful React changes and before opening a pull request.
Pin or review the npm package version in sensitive repositories.
Fix high-impact diagnostics first, then rerun the scan to confirm progress.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the score as a replacement for tests or human review.
Do not run unpinned external packages in restricted production environments.
Do not ignore diagnostics that affect security, correctness, or user-visible behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It runs React Doctor to scan a React codebase for security, performance, correctness, and architecture issues.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it modify my code automatically?
faq.answer: The skill description only documents scanning and diagnostics. Any fixes should be reviewed before applying changes.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The usage command runs an external npm package through npx with the latest tag, which creates supply-chain risk.

faq.question: How should I use it safely?
faq.answer: Use it in a development environment and consider pinning the react-doctor package version before running it.

faq.question: Can it replace a full test suite?
faq.answer: No. It is a diagnostic aid and should be used with tests, code review, and normal release checks.
